reason: This text is a narrative account of Naaman's healing from leprosy (2 Kings 5). While it contains references to past military raids by Arameans and captures the tension and drama of seeking healing, it does not contain any prophecy or announcement of an upcoming significant destruction affecting the general public by Yahweh. The text focuses on individual healing and personal moral consequences (Gehazi's punishment), not on forthcoming public disaster. The military context (Syrian raiding parties) is presented as background history, not as an anticipated future event.
